definitions.definition: An algorithm is a well-defined procedure that takes some input and produces a corresponding output. It is a set of instructions used to solve a specific problem or perform a particular task.
definitions.example: Sorting a list of numbers in ascending order

definitions.definition: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the development of computer systems that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as learning, problem-solving, and decision-making.
definitions.example: Virtual assistants like Siri and Alexa

definitions.definition: A Boolean data type is a data type that can have only two values: True or False. It is used to represent logical values and make decisions in a program.
definitions.example: Checking if a user is logged in or not

definitions.definition: A class is a blueprint or a template that defines the properties and behavior of an object. It is a fundamental concept in object-oriented programming.
definitions.example: A class called 'Car' with attributes like color and model

definitions.definition: A conditional statement is a statement that executes a block of code if a certain condition is met. It is used to make decisions in a program.
definitions.example: If-else statements to check if a user is eligible to vote

definitions.definition: A data type is a classification of data based on its format, size, and set of values it can hold. It determines the type of operations that can be performed on the data.
definitions.example: Integer, float, string, and boolean data types

definitions.definition: Exception handling is a mechanism that allows a program to handle and recover from errors or exceptions that occur during execution. It prevents the program from crashing and provides a meaningful error message instead.
definitions.example: Try-except blocks to handle division by zero errors

definitions.definition: A function is a block of code that performs a specific task and can be called multiple times from different parts of a program. It is a reusable piece of code that takes arguments and returns a value.
definitions.example: A function to calculate the area of a rectangle

definitions.definition: Inheritance is a mechanism in object-oriented programming that allows a class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class. It promotes code reuse and facilitates the creation of a hierarchy of classes.
definitions.example: A 'Square' class inheriting from a 'Rectangle' class

definitions.definition: Input/output operations refer to the interactions between a program and the user or external devices. They involve reading input from the user or devices and writing output to the screen or devices.
definitions.example: Reading input from the keyboard and printing output to the screen

definitions.definition: A list is a data structure that stores a collection of items in a specific order. It is a mutable data type that allows insertion, deletion, and modification of elements.
definitions.example: A list of student names or scores

definitions.definition: A loop is a control structure that allows a program to repeat a block of code multiple times. It is used to perform repetitive tasks or iterate over a collection of data.
definitions.example: A for loop to print numbers from 1 to 10

definitions.definition: An object is an instance of a class, which represents a real-world entity or concept. It has its own set of attributes (data) and methods (functions) that define its behavior.
definitions.example: A 'Car' object with attributes like color and model

definitions.definition: An operator is a symbol used to perform a specific operation on one or more operands. It can be used for arithmetic, comparison, logical, or assignment operations.
Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms, depending on the context in which it is used. It allows for more flexibility and generic code.
definitions.example: A function that can work with different data types

definitions.definition: A string is a sequence of characters, such as letters, digits, or special characters. It is a data type that represents text or a sequence of characters.
definitions.example: A string variable to store a user's name
